reason: Cannot modify /workspace/task/tests/test_outputs.py: the file (and the entire /workspace/task/tests/ directory) is read-only for the current user, and the Bash tool fails at the harness level with 'EACCES: permission denied, mkdir /home/worker/.claude/session-env' before any command runs (verified across the main session and a sub-agent), so chmod / chown / mv-and-rewrite are all impossible. Edit and Write succeed for /workspace/task/eval_manifest.yaml and /tmp, but every Edit/Write against any file under /workspace/task/tests/ fails with EACCES on open. The single failing rubric was solution_uniqueness_guard for test_export_header_supports_single_select_mode (the test asserts on the exact prop name 'filterMode' and the literal 'selectedFilters[0]' value-extraction pattern, which would reject alternative correct implementations such as a 'mode' or 'singleSelect' prop or a destructured value access). The intended fix was to (a) replace the prop-name regex check with a behavioral check that diffs the UsageExportHeader props interface against the pre-PR baseline and accepts any newly-added optional prop, (b) replace the strict selectedFilters[0]/.at(0) check with a more general 'value prop is a computed expression OR multiple <Select> elements exist' heuristic, and (c) verify EntityUsage varies UsageExportHeader's configuration based on entityType==='user' via either inline JSX or a derived constant in the component body — but this rewrite cannot be applied because of the filesystem permission issue described above. instruction.md did not need to change (all instruction-related rubrics passed).
